vol_description:
Databrary is pleased to share recordings of most of the invited program from the XXI International Congress on Infant Studies, held in Philadelphia, PA from June 30-July 3, 2018.

SATURDAY, JUNE 30

We’ve come a long way, baby! ICIS at 40 and beyond
Kim Boller, Ellen Galinsky

SUNDAY, JULY 1

Invited Speaker: Play in infancy: A foundation for development and learning
Victoria Leong, Justine Howard, Kathy Hirsh-Pasek, Roberta Golinkoff

Presidential symposium: The urgent work of bridging gaps between communications and the learning sciences
Lisa Guernsey, Brenda Jones Harden, Marcela Peña, Alison Wilder, Michael Yogman

Presidential Address: Building bridges: Why science, practice, journalism and policy should play together
Kathy Hirsh-Pasek, Roberta Golinkoff

MONDAY, JULY 2

Keynote speaker: The growth and prospects for continued increases in public support for early care and education programs
Ron Haskins

Invited speaker: Raising hurricanes: Insights from the science and complexity of child development
Yuko Munakata

Keynote speaker: Early language learning: A focus on mechanisms
Patricia Kuhl

Invited speaker: What makes the human infant mind so special: Insights from non-human animals
Laurie Santos

TUESDAY, JULY 3

Keynote speaker: From spoken to written language : Literacy and human brain plasticity
Stan Dehaene

Invited panel: Life after (and during) administration:  The costs and benefits of service to the field
Dick Aslin, Sarah Mangelsdorf, Laura Namy

Invited speaker: Developmental relational science into practice
Dick Aslin, Sarah Mangelsdorf, Laura Namy
